channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Kate returns to cancer center where she was treated to offer gratitude to medical teams
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The Princess of Wales visited the hospital where she was treated for cancer on Tuesday, offering gratitude to the medical teams who helped her and give support to patients now undergoing treatment.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Hamas has accepted draft agreement for Gaza cease-fire, hostage release: officials
item.description: Hamas has reportedly agreed to a draft of a multi-phase cease-fire deal with Israel that would include the release of hostages who were taken into Gaza on Oct. 7, 2023.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top aide of impeached South Korean president pleads for investigators to halt detention efforts
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The top aide of impeached South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol pleaded with law enforcement agencies on Tuesday to abandon their efforts to detain him over his imposition of martial law last month, as authorities prepared a second attempt to take him into custody.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: ICC chief prosecutor wants Israeli objections over Netanyahu warrant to be rejected
item.description: The International Criminal Court’s chief prosecutor has told judges that Israeli objections to the investigation into the 13-month war in Gaza should be rejected.
item.dc:creator: Molly Quell, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Zelenskyy, Macron discuss Western troop deployment in Ukraine before German defense chief visits
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says he has held further discussions with French President Emmanuel Macron about the possibility of Western troops deploying in Ukraine to safeguard any peace deal ending the nearly three-year war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Yehor Konovalov, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Millions of pilgrims and naked, ash-smeared ascetics take holy dips in India's mega Hindu festival
item.description: PRAYAGRAJ, India (AP) — Tens of thousands of naked Hindu ascetics and millions of pilgrims took dips in freezing water at the confluence of sacred rivers in northern India on Tuesday, in the first of a series of major baths in the Maha Kumbh festival, the largest religious congregation on Earth.
item.dc:creator: SHEIKH SAALIQ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ugandan military court rules it can try opposition figure on treachery charge carrying death penalty
item.description: KAMPALA, Uganda (AP) — A prominent opposition figure in Uganda will stand trial on the serious charge of treachery, a military court ruled Tuesday, escalating the legal trouble Kizza Besigye faces ahead of presidential elections scheduled for 2026.
item.dc:creator: RODNEY MUHUMUZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rescuers bring out survivors and dead from a South African mine as hundreds remain deep underground
item.description: Rescuers have sent a cage-like structure into one of South Africa’s deepest gold mines to bring out survivors among hundreds of miners working illegally in an abandoned shaft who have been trapped for months.
item.dc:creator: Mogomotsi Magome And Gerald Imray, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: European defense heavyweights say meeting Trump's military spending target won't be easy
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — The defense ministers of Europe's five top military spenders said Monday they want to continue increasing their investments in defense but described meeting President-elect Donald Trump's challenge for them to raise spending to 5% of their overall economic output as complicated
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA and MONIKA SCISLOWSKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea launches missiles toward eastern waters in 2nd launch of year, South says
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea on Tuesday test-fired multiple missiles toward its eastern waters, South Korea’s military said, as it continued its weapons demonstrations ahead of Donald Trump’s return to the White House.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas has accepted a draft agreement for a Gaza ceasefire and the release of hostages, officials say
item.description: Officials say Hamas has accepted a draft agreement for a ceasefire in the Gaza Strip and the release of dozens of hostages.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Head of the International Court of Justice is named Lebanon's new prime minister
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Lebanon’s new president has asked prominent diplomat and jurist Nawaf Salam to form the country’s new government after Salam was named prime minister by a large number of legislators Monday. The move apparently angered the Hezbollah group and its allies.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Gaza ceasefire talks press on in Qatar
item.description: Israel and Hamas appear to be inching closer to a phased ceasefire agreement, officials said Monday. However, a deal hasn't been reached yet and there are a number of obstacles that could still scupper the talks in Qatar. Israel's war against Hamas in Gaza began on Oct.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Court papers say ex-NBA player Jontay Porter laid out betting scheme in a text; 6th person arrested
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A sixth person was charged Monday in the federal sports betting case involving ex-NBA player Jontay Porter, and authorities disclosed a text message Porter allegedly sent explaining how to cash in on his plans to bench himself in a January 2024 game.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cease-fire between Israel and Hamas getting closer amid concerns terror group rearming in Gaza
item.description: Israel and Hamas are nearing a cease-fire deal amid escalating death toll; 5 IDF soldiers were killed in Gaza on Monday amid growing warnings that Hamas is rearming.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico sends firefighters to Los Angeles to help battle devastating wildfires
item.description: Disaster relief workers from the National Forestry Commission and the Ministry of Defense in Mexico also arrived to assist local emergency workers.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, NewsNation

item.title: Jake Sullivan: 'We are so close' to ceasefire, hostage deal in Gaza
item.description: White House national security adviser Jake Sullivan said Monday the U.S. is “so close to a ceasefire and hostage deal” in Gaza, as President Biden races to secure an agreement on a major foreign policy objective before leaving office in a week.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Italian photographer Oliviero Toscani, famed for provocative Benetton campaigns, dies at 82
item.description: MILAN (AP) — Oliviero Toscani, the photographer behind Benetton’s provocative ad campaigns of the 1980s and 1990s who later broke with the Italian knitwear brand amid controversy, died Monday at age 82. Toscani disclosed last year he had a rare disease.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY and GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former ambassador to Denmark: NATO would be compelled to respond to Greenland invasion
item.description: Rufus Gifford, a former U.S. ambassador to Denmark, said in a Sunday interview that the NATO alliance would be compelled to respond to any invasion or incursion into Greenland.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Watch live: Biden delivers foreign address at State Department
item.description: President Biden began his farewell tour around Washington this week with an address on Monday at the State Department to highlight his work on foreign policy during his presidency. Biden was expected to defend his foreign policy legacy, including reconnecting the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Hill

item.title: Tsunami alert lifted after magnitude 6.6 earthquake rattles southwestern Japan
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan issued a tsunami advisory on Monday after a magnitude 6.6 earthquake struck the southwest of the country. The public warnings to stay away from coastal areas were later lifted. The agency initially gave the magnitude estimate at 6.9, but later revised it down.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Russian forces bypass a key stronghold in a bid to cut off its supplies, a Ukrainian officer says
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian forces are bypassing a key stronghold in eastern Ukraine that they have fought for months to capture and are focusing instead on cutting supply lines to it, a Ukrainian official said Monday.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and YEHOR KONOVALOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Approval of US leadership among NATO members sinks: Gallup
item.description: Approval of U.S. leadership across NATO member countries was lower in 2024 than during the first three years of President Biden’s term in office, according to a Gallup poll released Monday. In 2024, the median approval of U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Seoul's spy agency says North Korean soldiers captured in Ukraine haven't shown desire to defect
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s spy agency told lawmakers on Monday that two North Korean soldiers who were captured by Ukrainian forces while fighting alongside Russian forces in Russia’s Kursk border region haven’t expressed a desire to seek asylum in South Korea.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 1 poll finds majority of Greenland respondents support joining US
item.description: A new survey found that a majority of Greenland respondents support joining the United States. According to a poll by Patriot Polling released Sunday, 57.3 percent of respondents approve of Greenland becoming part of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Ukraine has captured 2 North Korean soldiers, South Korea's intelligence service says
item.description: Ukraine captured two wounded North Korean soldiers who were fighting on behalf of Russia in a Russian border region, South Korea’s intelligence service said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia says it will continue oil and gas projects despite US sanctions
item.description: Russia's Foreign Ministry has denounced U.S. sanctions against Moscow's energy sector and said the country would carry on with large oil and gas projects.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden admin slammed for ‘waiting’ to declare genocide in Sudan
item.description: The incoming Chairman of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee has blasted the Biden Admnin for waiting until just 13 days before their term ends to declare atrocities in Sudan’s war as genocide.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lebanon's new president strikes a nationalistic tone amid regional shifts, further weakening of Hezbollah
item.description: The Lebanese parliament finally voted on a new president on Thursday following two years of deadlock. Joseph Aoun the head of the country's armed forces won a majority of the votes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Details of Venezuelan opposition leader's possible arrest remain unclear amid Maduro inauguration resistance
item.description: Venezuelan opposition leader Maria Corina Machado's aides claim she was detained Thursday, despite denials by the country's socialist government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cold spell claims first two lives in El Paso-Juarez area
item.description: Tuesday’s death of a homeless man presumably due to hypothermia has set off alarms in a border city with a large floating population of migrants.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Jean-Marie Le Pen, French far-right leader known for fiery rhetoric against immigration, dies at 96
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Jean-Marie Le Pen, the founder of France’s far-right National Front who was known for fiery rhetoric against immigration and multiculturalism that earned him both staunch supporters and widespread condemnation, died Tuesday. He was 96.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON, SYLVIE CORBET and ELAINE GANLEY, Associated Press, WGN 9
